architecture.summary: Self-BioRAG is a retrieval-augmented generation framework for biomedical question-answering that combines an LLM backbone with a biomedical document retriever, a biomedical corpus, and domain-specific instruction tuning. The system generates explanations, retrieves domain-specific documents conditionally, and applies self-reflection via customized reflective tokens to assess generated responses before final output. Information flows from the input question through the LLM, which decides whether to trigger retrieval, then incorporates retrieved documents and self-reflection scores back into generation.

biblio.title: Improving medical reasoning through retrieval and self-reflection with retrieval-augmented large language models

claims.statement: Self-BioRAG achieves a 7.2% absolute improvement on average over the state-of-the-art open-foundation model with a parameter size of 7B or less on three major medical question-answering benchmark datasets
Self-BioRAG outperforms RAG by 8% Rouge-1 score in generating more proficient answers on two long-form question-answering benchmarks on average
Self-BioRAG can assess its generated explanations with customized reflective tokens

Self-BioRAG finds the clues in the question, retrieves relevant documents if needed, and understands how to answer with information from retrieved documents and encoded knowledge as a medical expert does
Domain-specific components, such as a retriever, domain-related document corpus, and instruction sets are necessary for adhering to domain-related instructions
Self-BioRAG is trained on 84k filtered biomedical instruction sets
